_meta.description: R2B artist-name diagnostic: Luke Combs (Modern country). Artist named in caption. 1 seed. Locked 2026-05-17.

captions.ace_step: Modern country song in the style of Luke Combs, 88 BPM, mid-tempo radio-friendly arrangement, strummed acoustic guitar, pedal steel, fiddle, male baritone vocal with Southern rasp, nostalgic sincere mood, polished Nashville production

lyrics:
[Intro]

[Verse]
Tail lights fading down the county road
Pickup empty where she used to be
Mama's wedding ring still on the dresser
Heaven sometimes looks a lot like leaving

[Pre-Chorus]
And I keep telling everyone I'm alright
Telling everybody time will heal

[Chorus]
But the front porch swing won't swing the same
The kitchen radio knows her name
I built this house with both our hands
Now I'm sleeping in the wreckage of our plans
She took the dog, she took the years
She left me everything I can't bear

[Verse]
Sister called and asked if I'd been eating
I told her yeah, then I poured another drink
Pastor said come Sunday I should join him
I been singing to myself a little less each week

[Chorus]
But the front porch swing won't swing the same
The kitchen radio knows her name
I built this house with both our hands
Now I'm sleeping in the wreckage of our plans
